title: Lucid names auto industry outsider as CEO, expands Uber deal
text: Lucid Names New CEO and Expands Uber Deal. CEO Appointment. Lucid Motors appointed Silvio Napoli, a veteran of Schindler Group, as its new permanent CEO after a lengthy search. Napoli spent nearly 31 years at Schindler as chairman and CEO of the industrial manufacturer of escalators and elevators, making him the company's second CEO and first non-automotive industry veteran to lead the company. Strategic Shift. Lucid paired the new CEO with $750 million in fresh capital, signaling that scale and profitability now outrank brand-building. When asked about whether his reputation for cost discipline would lead him to review Lucid's existing projects and narrow focus to core business, Napoli indicated this was a high priority. Investment Details. Lucid secured $750 million in new investment from Ayar Third Investment and Uber, with Ayar buying $550 million of convertible preferred stock and Uber adding $200 million. Robotaxi Expansion. The companies also expanded their robotaxi commitment to at least 35,000 Lucid vehicles, including the Gravity SUV and future midsize models, designed exclusively for Uber's robotaxi service.
